ratings.comment: Currently in the class, I have to watch the recorded lectures 3 times over just to understand what he is saying most of the time. I think he's a nice guy, but if you any problems understanding accents at all, avoid taking his classes.

Prof Masson is one of the BEST professors I've had so far. He's a great lecturer and goes at a decent pace. His exams are just like the practice and you don't need read the book or do the problems to do well. He is accessible out side of class and is the nicest and really wants you to learn the material. I wish he taught the whole section.

Dr. Masson is a great lecturer that takes the time to make it clear to students what they need to know and what's expected of them. The practice problems and quizzes are pretty reflective of his exams, so make sure you understand them!

I had professor Masson for the middle section of the course. He was a HUGE improvement over the first professor, and actually seemed interested in teaching. His exams were tough, but I think that is consistent with 466 as a course. He was always sure to ask if there were any questions, and encouraged class discussions, even during our big lecture.
He has a great sense of humor, and I can't really picture someone making it more fun since it Genetics 466. But sometimes he's unclear.

Professor Masson has a Canadian/French accent that makes him difficult to understand, material covered in lecture is important, but he doesnt begin to cover the types of problems that will be on the exam. READ THE BOOK AND DO THE PROBLEMS or this class will tear you apart. Exams are   1000X more difficult if you don't do the problems in the book.
He's a really cool guy. Very funny. He explains things well. Tests difficult and seem to capture the things you don't know. I would recommend him.
A really nice man that is best suited for research. The worst slides I've ever seen combined dull material (biochem) is a killer.
Goes really fast and extremely hard to understand with the strong French accent. Sees very enthusiastic about the material, but still the accent makes it hard to follow.
